title: 10 best herbal decoctions for psoriasis
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for psoriasis are a traditional remedy that has been used to alleviate symptoms of the chronic skin condition. These decoctions are made by steeping herbs in hot water, releasing their active compounds and anti-inflammatory properties. Turmeric, chamomile, and oatmeal decoctions have shown promise in reducing redness, itching, and scaling associated with psoriasis. By using these herbal decoctions, individuals with psoriasis can experience relief from discomfort and embarrassment, allowing them to live more confidently and comfortably.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe decoctions helps with psoriasis because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and redness associated with the condition. The decoctions also have antioxidant properties, which help to repair damaged skin cells and promote collagen production, leading to improved skin elasticity and flexibility. Additionally, aloe vera has been shown to modulate the immune system, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of psoriasis. By using herbal aloe decoctions topically, individuals with psoriasis may experience relief from itching, scaling, and redness, promoting healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal aloe decoctions have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the skin, which is a hallmark symptom of psoriasis. This reduces redness, swelling, and pain associated with the condition.

Antioxidant property: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms and promote chronic inflammation.

Immunomodulatory property: Herbal aloe decoctions contain immunomodulatory compounds that help regulate the immune system's response to psoriasis, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting a balanced immune response.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in aloin, a compound that helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, making it effective in treating psoriasis symptoms.
Leaves: Containing high amounts of vitamin E and other antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin cell growth.
remedy_recipe: Wash and chop fresh aloe vera leaves into small pieces weighing about one hundred fifty grams.
Combine the chopped aloe vera with two liters of distilled water in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for twenty-five minutes or until the liquid reduces to one liter.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in the refrigerator at a temperature of three degrees celsius for up to five days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with psoriasis because they possess potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and calm irritated skin. The decoction's rich concentration of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and carotenoids works to reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriatic lesions, while its antifungal and antibacterial properties combat underlying infections that may exacerbate the condition. Additionally, pot marigold decoctions have been shown to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation in skin tissue, making them a valuable natural remedy for managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: The flavonoids and terpenes present in calendula decoctions can reduce inflammation, which is a primary symptom of psoriasis.
Antioxidant activity: Calendula's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from oxidative stress, damage caused by free radicals, and DNA mutations that contribute to psoriasis development.

Wound-healing and antimicrobial effects: The decoction's ability to promote wound healing and inhibit microbial growth may aid in reducing symptoms of psoriasis, such as itching, scaling, and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in psoriasis patients.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the pot marigold plant have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and are believed to help reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried calendula flowers for each day's treatment.
Rinse the dried flowers with boiling water to rehydrate them for 10-15 minutes.
Combine the rehydrated flowers with 500ml of distilled water in a glass jar for steeping.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 30-45 minutes then strain and discard solids.
Use the resulting clear decoction as a topical treatment applying 20-25ml to affected areas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric decoctions helps with psoriasis because it contains curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. This natural remedy has been shown to improve skin health by reducing symptoms such as redness, itching, and scaling associated with psoriasis. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of turmeric also help to reduce joint pain and stiffness, common comorbidities with psoriasis. By incorporating turmeric decoctions into a daily routine, individuals with psoriasis may experience improved overall skin health and reduced symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Turmeric contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds like curcumin, which reduces inflammation and prevents further damage to skin tissues, helping to alleviate symptoms of psoriasis.

Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from oxidative stress and environmental factors that can trigger or exacerbate psoriasis, promoting healthy skin cell function and reducing signs of the condition.
Immune system modulation: Turmeric has been shown to modulate the immune system, reducing overactive immune responses that contribute to psoriasis symptoms, such as redness, scaling, and itching.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with psoriasis.
Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like curcuminoids, which have been shown to possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the washed turmeric roots in a warm oven at 150°f for 24 hours.
Grind 10g of dried turmeric powder into fine particles using a spice grinder.
Steep 5g of ground turmeric powder in 500ml of boiling distilled water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, allowing the liquid to cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with psoriasis because of its unique composition of antioxidants, flavonoids, and saponins. These compounds work together to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common triggers of the condition. The decoction also possesses anti-proliferative properties, which help to slow down the rapid skin cell growth associated with psoriasis. Additionally, the decoction's moisturizing and soothing effects can help to calm redness and itchiness, providing relief from symptoms and promoting a healthy, clear complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants and polyphenols may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin, which can contribute to psoriasis symptoms.

Immunomodulatory properties: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to modulate the immune system by reducing pro-inflammatory cytokines and increasing anti-inflammatory cytokines, which could potentially help alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
Antiproliferative effects: Ginkgo biloba's compounds may inhibit the growth of skin cells that contribute to psoriasis lesions, helping to reduce their size and severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree are used to make decoctions that help reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis, while also promoting skin healing.
Bark: The bark of the maidenhair tree contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing redness and swelling caused by psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and two cups of pure water.
Bring the mixture to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan over high heat for three minutes.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng decoctions helps with psoriasis because of its ability to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common underlying factors in the disease. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe itchy and irritated skin, while its antioxidant compounds can combat free radicals that contribute to cell damage. Additionally, Siberian ginseng has been shown to modulate the immune system, reducing excessive inflammation and promoting a balanced response. By addressing these underlying causes, herbal Siberian ginseng decoctions may provide relief from psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Siberian Ginseng decoctions contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of psoriasis symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with psoriasis, making it a potential natural remedy for soothing skin irritations.

Immune system modulation: Siberian Ginseng has been shown to regulate immune cell activity, which can help mitigate the overactive immune response that contributes to psoriasis development and exacerbation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve skin health.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes have anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming the skin affected by psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 grams of dried eleutherococcus senticosus root, rinsed clean from any debris.
Measure 10 cups of pure water and pour it over the herbs in a large pot, ensuring proper coverage.
Bring the mixture to a boil on high heat, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 1 hour.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool, then refrigerate it at 39°f to 45°f for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort decoctions helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The decoction has been shown to reduce inflammation in the skin, which is a hallmark symptom of psoriasis. Additionally, St John's wort has been found to modulate the immune system, reducing the activation of T-cells that contribute to the development of psoriatic lesions. By calming the inflammatory response and regulating the immune system, St John's wort decoctions can help alleviate symptoms such as redness, itching, and scaling associated with psoriasis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort has been shown to have potent antioxidant effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, common underlying factors in psoriasis.

Anti-inflammatory activity: The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with psoriasis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Immunomodulatory effects: St John's Wort has been found to modulate immune responses, which can help regulate an overactive immune system that contributes to psoriasis symptoms such as plaque formation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St. John's Wort contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and improve skin health.

Barks: The barks of St. John's Wort contain hyperforin, a compound known for its ability to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, making it an effective ingredient in medicinal decoctions for psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves according to traditional guidelines.
Measure out two teaspoons of dried hypericum perforatum per cup of cold water.
Combine the dried herbs and water in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup or pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discard solids and let cool to room temperature before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal decoctions helps with psoriasis because its active compounds, berberine and isoquinoline alkaloids, have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the skin, which can alleviate symptoms of psoriasis such as redness, swelling, and itching. Additionally, goldenseal's antibacterial and antifungal properties may help to combat infections that can exacerbate psoriasis, promoting a healthier skin environment and reducing the occurrence of flares.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent infections that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms.

Benzylisoquinoline Alkaloids (BIAs): Possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and regulate the immune response to reduce psoriasis symptoms.

Berberastin: Exhibits anti-proliferative and pro-apoptotic properties, which can help reduce the rapid growth of skin cells that is characteristic of psoriasis and induce programmed cell death (apoptosis) in affected cells.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm irritated skin.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of alkaloids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Rinse dried goldenseal root powder with 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Mix 10-15 grams of hydrastis canadensis root powder with 2 cups of cold distilled water.
Simmer the mixture in a double boiler at 100°f to 110°f for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Store the cooled and filtered decoction in an amber glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm decoctions helps with psoriasis because its mucilaginous properties soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and redness. The decoction's emollient effects also help to moisturize and soften dry, scaly skin patches, promoting a healthier skin barrier. Additionally, slippery elm contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce the production of cytokines, which contribute to psoriasis flares. By providing gentle, non-irritating relief, slippery elm decoctions can be a valuable complementary therapy for managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with psoriatic lesions.
Sotheing and protective mucilages: The mucilage content in slippery elm helps soothe and protect the skin from further irritation, reducing itching and discomfort caused by psoriasis.
Immune system modulation: Slippery elm's immunomodulatory effects can help regulate an overactive immune response, which is believed to contribute to the development of psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of mucilages, which help to soothe and moisturize the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with psoriasis.
Barks: The barks contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that aid in reducing redness, swelling, and itching caused by psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra bark or dry it for future use.
Cut and chop the bark into small pieces.
Combine the chopped bark with water in a pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain and store the decoction in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with the condition. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root also helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting a healthy environment for healing. Additionally, licorice decoctions have been shown to improve skin elasticity, firmness, and hydration, which can help to reduce psoriasis symptoms such as dryness and scaling. As a result, using herbal licorice decoctions may provide relief from psoriasis-related discomfort and promote overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice root has been shown to reduce inflammation, which is a common underlying factor in psoriasis, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
Immunomodulatory property: Licorice root has immunosuppressive effects, which can help modulate an overactive immune response that can contribute to psoriasis symptoms such as redness and irritation.

Antioxidant property: Licorice root is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms like itching, scaling, and thickened skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making it effective in reducing redness and irritation associated with psoriasis.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing, making them beneficial for treating psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in quantities of 10-15 grams for each day's dose.
Rinse the dried roots thoroughly with distilled water to remove any impurities.
Chop the roots into small pieces and transfer them to a heat-resistant container.
Combine one part chopped root with eight parts boiling water and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with psoriasis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpene lactones, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the skin, thus alleviating symptoms like redness, itching, and scaling. Additionally, dandelion decoctions may help modulate the immune response, which is often disrupted in psoriasis patients. By soothing and calming the skin, these natural extracts can provide relief from this chronic condition, promoting a clearer, healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and redness associated with psoriasis, promoting healing and skin calmness.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in dandelion decoction neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, reducing oxidative stress and helping to prevent further psoriasis flares.

Laxative properties: Dandelion's natural laxative effects help remove toxins and excess waste from the body, which can contribute to psoriasis symptoms by reducing inflammation and improving overall gut health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are valued for their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of psoriasis, such as redness and scaling.

Seeds: The seeds of the dandelion plant contain a compound called taraxasterol, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe skin irritation associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality taraxacum officinale roots and rhizomes of 2-3 year-old plants.
Cleanly wash the roots and rhizomes with cold running water to remove any debris or dirt.
Chop the cleaned roots and rhizomes into small pieces, then macerate them in a ratio of 1 part herb to 4 parts water for 30 minutes.
Bring the macerated mixture to a boil in a large pot, then reduce heat and simmer for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover decoctions helps with psoriasis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with this chronic skin condition. The decoction's high concentration of isoflavones also inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory molecules, alleviating symptoms such as itching and scaling. Additionally, red clover's antioxidant activity helps to neutralize free radicals, promoting healthy skin cell growth and reducing oxidative stress that can exacerbate psoriasis. As a result, regular use of red clover decoctions may help alleviate psoriasis symptoms, promoting clear and healthy-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover's high content of isoflavones, such as formononetin and biochanin A, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with psoriasis.

Saponins: The saponins present in red clover decoctions have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory properties, which can help regulate the immune system's response to triggers that exacerbate psoriasis symptoms.

Polyphenols: Red clover's polyphenolic compounds, such as quercetin and kaempferol, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help reduce skin inflammation, prevent DNA damage, and protect against skin cell damage caused by UV light exposure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Red clover roots have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions like psoriasis due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Buds: The buds of the red clover plant are rich in flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in skin conditions like psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh trifolium pratense flowers or dried aerial parts and store them in an airtight container.
Measure one teaspoon of dried trifolium pratense aerial parts per cup of boiling water and add it to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar discarding solids.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The decoction's antioxidant compounds also help to reduce redness and swelling, making it an effective natural remedy for managing symptoms of psoriasis. Additionally, chamomile's gentle astringent properties can help to reduce flaking and scaling, promoting clearer and healthier-looking skin. By incorporating chamomile decoctions into one's skincare routine, individuals with psoriasis may experience improved symptom management and overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with psoriatic lesions.
Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidant compounds scavenge free radicals that can damage skin cells and exacerbate psoriasis symptoms, promoting a healthier and more resilient skin barrier.

Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenoids have a calming effect on the skin, reducing itching, irritation, and discomfort associated with psoriasis, while also promoting relaxation and stress relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are a popular choice for making decoctions due to their high concentration of apigenin, an antioxidant that helps soothe and calm irritated skin.
Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant contain bisabolol, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Measure accurately 10-15 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and place them in a heat-resistant container.
Combine the measured matricaria chamomilla flowers with 500 milliliters of boiling water and let it steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids from the liquid.
Transfer the filtered decoction to a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator at a temperature below 40 degrees fahrenheit.
Drink 250 milliliters of the prepared decoction thrice daily, ideally on an empty stomach, for a minimum duration of three consecutive weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle decoctions helps with psoriasis because its unique blend of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and flavonoids reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin. The decoction's potent antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms, such as redness and itching. Additionally, roselle's anti-inflammatory effects may help reduce plaque formation and scaling associated with psoriasis, promoting a clearer, healthier complexion. By soothing and calming the skin, roselle decoctions provide natural relief from psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Roselle decoctions have been shown to reduce inflammation, which is a key component of psoriasis, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals and promoting the release of anti-inflammatory cytokines.

Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content in Roselle decoctions helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to the development of psoriasis lesions, promoting healthier skin and reducing oxidative stress.

Wound healing promotion: Roselle decoctions have been found to enhance wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue repair, and reducing inflammation, which can help to alleviate symptoms of psoriasis such as skin thickening and scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing irritated skin and reducing inflammation associated with psoriasis.
Barks: Containing bioactive compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, beneficial for psoriasis treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hibiscus sabdariffa flowers and leaves for use in the decoction.
Combine one tablespoon of dried hibiscus sabdariffa flowers and one tablespoon of dried hibiscus sabdariffa leaves in a clean glass jar.
Pour two cups of boiling water over the herb mixture and let steep for 15 minutes to allow for maximum extraction.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate container discarding the solids and retaining only the liquid.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for refrigeration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with psoriasis because they possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in elderberries have been shown to reduce inflammation, a key factor in psoriasis. Additionally, the decoction's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate skin conditions. By soothing and calming the skin, elder decoctions can help alleviate symptoms of psoriasis such as redness, itching, and scaling, promoting healthy and clear skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Elderberry contains flavonoids, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids that exhibit potent anti-inflammatory activity, which helps reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with psoriatic lesions.

Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content in elderberry decoctions neutralizes free radicals and reduces oxidative stress, thereby minimizing damage to skin cells and reducing the severity of psoriasis symptoms such as scaling and flaking.

Immunomodulatory effects: Elderberry has been shown to modulate the immune response, which can help reduce the excessive inflammation and cell proliferation that characterizes psoriasis, ultimately leading to improved symptom control and reduced disease activity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, elder roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of psoriasis.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, elder rhyzomes possess potent antiproliferative effects, which can help regulate skin cell growth and reduce psoriasis lesions.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves from a trusted source, weighing 2 ounces per quart of water.
Combine the dried sambucus nigra with boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container, steeping for 15 minutes to allow infusion.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Bring the reserved liquid to a simmer over low heat, reducing volume by half to concentrate the herbs, taking approximately 20-30 minutes.
Cool the concentrated decoction to room temperature before storing it in a clean glass bottle for use as needed, keeping refrigerated for up to 7 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark decoctions helps with psoriasis because it contains high levels of hydroquinone, a compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and slow down cell growth. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate the redness and swelling associated with psoriasis, while its ability to inhibit cell growth can reduce scaling and plaque formation. Additionally, the decoction's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall skin health and reducing the severity of psoriatic lesions.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Jesuit's Bark decoctions have antimicrobial properties that help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in psoriatic lesions, promoting healing and reducing the severity of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as quinine and quinoline alkaloids, help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with psoriasis, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antioxidant properties: Jesuit's Bark decoctions are rich in antioxidants that help to neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by chronic inflammation, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks of the Jesuit's Bark plant contain cinchonine and quinine, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of psoriasis such as redness and swelling.
Roots: The roots of the Jesuit's Bark plant are rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that help reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried cinchona officinalis bark per cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes to allow the alkaloids and flavonoids to infuse.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Store the decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 48 hours.
Take two cups of the prepared decoction three times daily to alleviate psoriasis symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow decoctions helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and redness in affected areas. The decoction's salicylic acid content also helps to exfoliate and soften scales, promoting a smoother skin texture. Additionally, white willow's natural analgesic properties alleviate itching and discomfort associated with psoriasis, providing relief and improving overall skin health. By reducing inflammation and soothing irritated skin, herbal white willow decoctions can help to manage the symptoms of psoriasis.
remedy_properties: Salicin: Salicin, a natural pain-reliever similar to aspirin, helps reduce inflammation and relieve itching and discomfort associated with psoriasis.
Tannins: Tannins in white willow bark have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling caused by psoriasis.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in white willow decoctions have been shown to have antiproliferative effects, which means they may help slow down the rapid cell growth associated with psoriasis, leading to improved symptom management.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis.
Barks: Contain salicin and other bioactive compounds that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and itching caused by psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried white willow bark, containing 10-15% salicin.
Combine the dried herb with 500ml of cold water in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 8 hours at room temperature or overnight in the refrigerator.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Discard the solids and store the clear liquid decoction in an amber glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape decoctions helps with psoriasis because they provide anti-inflammatory properties that soothe itchy, scaly skin. The antioxidants present in these decoctions also help to reduce oxidative stress, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms. Additionally, grape decoctions are rich in flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, further reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with psoriasis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal grape decoctions contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which are common underlying factors in psoriasis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in herbal grape decoctions, such as flavonoids and anthocyanins, can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with psoriasis.

Antimicrobial properties: Herbal grape decoctions contain antimicrobial agents that may help prevent bacterial infections and promote healing of skin lesions, which is particularly important for individuals with psoriasis who are prone to skin infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the grape plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing skin inflammation and promoting healthy skin cell growth.
Barks: The barks of the grape plant contain tannins, which help to reduce skin irritation and itching associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh vitis vinifera leaves and flowers in adequate quantity for the decoction.
Rinse the gathered herbs thoroughly with cold running water to remove dirt and impurities.
Combine one tablespoon of dried vitis vinifera leaves and one teaspoon of dried flowers with two cups of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Store the decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days and consume one cup twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop decoctions helps with psoriasis because it targets the underlying inflammation that triggers the condition. The herb's flavonoids and saponins have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with psoriasis. Additionally, maypop's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals, promoting cellular health and reducing oxidative stress. By addressing these root causes, maypop decoctions can provide relief from psoriasis symptoms and promote healthy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Maypop decoctions contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis.

Antioxidant: The rich antioxidant content in maypop decoctions helps to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate skin damage and inflammation caused by psoriasis, promoting overall skin health and well-being.

Immunomodulatory: Maypop's immunomodulatory effects can help regulate the immune system, reducing excessive inflammation and preventing it from attacking healthy skin cells, which is a hallmark of psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maypop roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis.
Fruits: Maypop fruits contain antioxidants and fatty acids that can help soothe and moisturize skin affected by psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers in amounts of 20-30 grams for every 500ml of water needed.
Clean the harvested material by removing any stems leaves and debris gently and thoroughly rinse with distilled water.
Combine the cleaned passiflora incarnata flowers with boiling distilled water in a large glass jar or pot to create a maceration.
Allow the mixture to steep for 20-30 minutes then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Transfer the filtered decoction to an amber glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator at a temperature between 2-8 degrees celsius.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian decoctions helps with psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and redness, alleviating symptoms of the condition. The herb's antiseptic properties also help to prevent infection, promoting healthy skin cell growth and reducing flaking. Additionally, valerian root has been shown to stimulate the immune system, which can help to calm down an overactive response that contributes to psoriasis inflammation and lesions. Overall, incorporating valerian decoctions into a treatment plan may provide relief from psoriasis symptoms and promote overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian root has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate itching associated with psoriasis by calming the skin and reducing immune response.
Antioxidant properties: Valerian root's antioxidant activity helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, leading to psoriasis symptoms such as redness, scaling, and flaking.

Immunomodulatory effects: Valerian root has been found to modulate the immune system, reducing the overactive response associated with autoimmune diseases like psoriasis, and promoting a more balanced immune response.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots are rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and promote healing in psoriasis patients.

Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has been shown to have potent anti-itching and anti-inflammatory effects, making it an effective treatment for skin conditions like psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots and wash them gently under cold running water.
Chop the cleaned valeriana officinalis roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction.
Combine one teaspoon of chopped valeriana officinalis root with two cups of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid reaches your desired strength.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle decoctions helps with psoriasis because they possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with the condition. Barks: The barks of the stinging nettle plant contain triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties, helping to reduce symptoms of psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried stinging nettle leaves and stems in a clean glass container.
Boil 4 cups of pure water in a large pot over high heat for exactly 15 minutes.
Add the dried urtica dioica mixture to the boiling water and allow it to steep for 25 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass jar discarding solids immediately.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and take 1/2 cup twice daily as needed.

remedy_recipe: Measure out one teaspoon of dried althaea officinalis root per cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured althaea officinalis root and boiling water in a clean glass saucepan.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 10-15 minutes to extract beneficial compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large ceramic cup.
Allow the decoction to cool before drinking, with a recommended dosage of 2 cups per day.

remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried taraxacum mongolicum root powder and two cups of water.
Combine the powder with boiling water in a saucepan and reduce heat to low for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to three days.
Warm the decoction by gently heating it on low heat before consuming one cup twice daily.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa petals and rinse them gently under cold running water.
Combine 20 grams of dried rosa rugosa flowers with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a pot.
Reduce heat to a simmer for 15-20 minutes allowing the mixture to steep and extract active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea angustifolia roots measure one ounce of the herb.
Clean and prepare the harvested roots by cutting them into small pieces.
Combine the prepared roots with four cups of water in a medium-sized saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried taraxacum kok saghyz root and 10g of dried gentiana scabra herb.
Grind the combined herbs into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 20g of the powdered herb mixture with 500ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 30 minutes, then strain through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days at a dosage of 2 tablespoons twice daily.

remedy_recipe: Harvest alcea rosea flowers and leaves from a trusted source or store-bought dried form in sufficient quantity for one month's supply.
Combine one tablespoon of dried alcea rosea flowers and leaves with two cups of distilled water in a large glass pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil and then reduce heat to low for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid reaches a thickness of one cup.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids and retaining the clear liquid.

Store the herbal decoction in the refrigerator at a temperature below 40°f for up to seven days or freeze for later use by transferring it to an ice cube tray then placing the frozen cubes in a freezer-safe bag.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borago officinalis leaves and stems, rinsing them gently with clean water.
Chop the herb into small pieces, measuring out 10-20 grams for a single serving.
Combine the chopped herb with 500ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15-20 minutes, then strain and discard solids using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Store the decoction in an amber glass bottle in the refrigerator, using within 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers for every cup of boiling water needed.
Combine the measured herb and water in a saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat quickly.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes slowly.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container carefully.
Let the cooled decoction steep in the refrigerator for at least 24 hours before using daily.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat psoriasis with herbal decoctions is by brewing a strong tea from a combination of turmeric, ginger, and licorice root. This blend has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce redness and itching associated with the condition. Boil 1-2 teaspoons of dried herbs in 1 cup of water for 10-15 minutes, then strain and drink 2-3 times a day to alleviate symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to psoriasis that are treated with herbal decoctions are eczema, dermatitis, and vitiligo. These conditions, characterized by skin inflammation and discoloration, can be alleviated with the use of herbal remedies like neem, turmeric, and aloe vera. Decoctions made from these herbs have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe itchy skin, reduce redness, and promote healing. Additionally, decoctions may also be used to treat other skin conditions such as acne, rosacea, and minor wounds.
